task: Clone rlp-dashboard repo, build beautiful real-time RLP mission control web app with bidirectional sync to local rlp-state.json, deploy on Netlify with permanent URL, auto-reconnect when PC back online, full task editing from anywhere + Make https://megatodo-michaelunkai.netlify.app/ have every Todoist feature with permanent Supabase database, import all Todoist data permanently
context: Project 1 (RLP Dashboard): GitHub repo https://github.com/Michaelunkai/rlp-dashboard. Local RLP state: C:\Users\micha\.claude\workspace\rlp-state.json. Tech: React/Vite, Tailwind, Netlify Functions, PS v5 sync agent. Project 2 (MegaTodo): GitHub repo https://github.com/Michaelunkai/megatodo. Deployed: https://megatodo-michaelunkai.netlify.app/. Goal: feature-parity with Todoist (projects, sections, tasks, labels, priorities, due dates, recurring tasks, reminders, filters, board/list view, quick add, search, comments, attachments, karma stats, import/export). Database: Supabase (permanent cloud DB). Auth: Supabase Auth. Import user Todoist data permanently. Force push to main, redeploy same URL, update README. CRITICAL: PS v5 syntax only.

missionExplanations.RLP Dashboard:
GOAL: Build and deploy a stunning, real-time web dashboard for Claude Code RLP mission control that syncs bidirectionally with the local rlp-state.json file, accessible from anywhere at any time.

The RLP Dashboard is a web application hosted on Netlify that provides full visibility and control over the Ralph Loop Plus (RLP) automated todo system used by Claude Code. The local machine stores RLP state in C:\Users\micha\.claude\workspace\rlp-state.json, which contains missions, todos, progress, and context. This dashboard reads that state, displays it beautifully, and allows the user to edit tasks, reorder them, add new ones, mark them done, and manage missions -- all from a browser on any device.

The architecture has three layers: (1) A React/Next.js frontend deployed as a static site on Netlify with a permanent custom subdomain URL. The UI must be world-class -- dark theme, glassmorphism cards, smooth animations, progress rings, drag-and-drop reordering, inline editing, mission grouping with expandable sections, and mobile-responsive design. (2) A Netlify Functions (serverless) backend that stores the latest state in a cloud-accessible location and serves as the sync endpoint. It accepts POST requests with updated state from the local PC and GET requests from the web UI. (3) A local PowerShell sync agent that runs on the user's PC (via Task Scheduler or startup script) and periodically pushes rlp-state.json to the cloud endpoint, and pulls any web-originated edits back to the local file.

When the PC is off, the web app continues to work with the last-synced state stored in the serverless backend. Users can still edit, reorder, and manage tasks. These changes are queued. When the PC comes back online, the sync agent detects it has been offline, pulls all pending web changes, merges them with local state (web edits win for conflicts on the same todo), and pushes the merged state back.

GitHub repo: https://github.com/Michaelunkai/rlp-dashboard
Deployed: TBD (will be set up during this mission)

Key files: package.json, src/App.jsx (or pages/index.tsx), netlify/functions/sync.js, public/index.html, netlify.toml, and the local sync script at C:\Users\micha\.claude\scripts\rlp-sync-agent.ps1. The sync agent must use PS v5 compatible syntax (semicolons not &&, no em-dashes, ConvertFrom-Json/ConvertTo-Json).
missionExplanations.MegaTodo Todoist Clone:
GOAL: Transform https://megatodo-michaelunkai.netlify.app/ into a fully-featured Todoist clone with permanent Supabase database, every Todoist feature without exception, and all the user's existing Todoist data imported permanently.

The MegaTodo project lives at https://github.com/Michaelunkai/megatodo and is deployed at https://megatodo-michaelunkai.netlify.app/. The mission is to make it feature-complete compared to Todoist -- the gold standard task management app. Currently the app likely uses localStorage or has limited features. The permanent database must be Supabase (PostgreSQL), so every task, project, label, comment, and attachment persists across devices and sessions forever.

The Todoist feature set to implement (every single one, no exceptions):
- Projects with color coding, icons, sharing/collaboration
- Sections within projects (grouping tasks)
- Tasks with: title, description/notes, due date+time, priority (P1/P2/P3/P4), labels, assignee, project/section, parent task (subtasks)
- Recurring tasks (every day, week, month, custom RRULE patterns)
- Labels/tags with colors (user-defined)
- Filters (custom saved filter views with boolean logic)
- Inbox (default project for unorganized tasks)
- Today view (all tasks due today or overdue)
- Upcoming view (7-day calendar of due tasks)
- Board/Kanban view (drag cards between sections)
- List view (default flat list)
- Quick Add (keyboard shortcut Q or + with smart date/label parsing)
- Global search (search all tasks, projects, comments)
- Comments on tasks (threaded)
- File attachments (stored in Supabase Storage)
- Reminders/notifications (browser push notifications + email)
- Keyboard shortcuts (full Todoist-compatible set: Q=quick add, A=add below, p=priority, d=date, @=label, #=project, /=search)
- Productivity stats / Karma system (daily streaks, tasks completed per day/week, trend charts)
- Goals (daily/weekly targets)
- Import from Todoist (JSON export format: parse projects, tasks, labels, due dates)
- Export functionality (JSON + CSV)
- Mobile-responsive design (works on phone)
- Dark/light theme toggle

Database schema: projects, sections, tasks, labels, task_labels, comments, attachments, filters, reminders, user_stats tables in Supabase.

Auth: Supabase Auth (email/password + optionally Google OAuth).

The user's existing Todoist data must be imported permanently into Supabase -- all projects, tasks, labels, due dates, priorities, and notes.

GitHub repo: https://github.com/Michaelunkai/megatodo
Deployed: https://megatodo-michaelunkai.netlify.app/

After completing all features, force push to main branch and redeploy to the SAME Netlify URL. Update README.md with the permanent URL and feature list. The URL must remain https://megatodo-michaelunkai.netlify.app/ -- do not create a new site.
